title: pyrus × proctoriana
intro: Pyrus × proctoriana, also known as the Proctor's pear, is a rare and hybrid species of pear tree, resulting from a cross between Pyrus communis and Pyrus calleryana. The plant's bark, leaves, and seeds contain medicinal properties, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits associated with Pyrus × proctoriana include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, pain, and digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as in traditional medicine formulations, although its usage may vary depending on the region and cultural practices.
